cards.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Embed 8B Takes #1 on RTEB Leaderboard with 32k-Context, NVFP4 Deployment
cards.why_it_matters: As an AI/ML engineer working with RAG or agentic systems, this is a direct SOTA embedding model drop with open weights, production-ready tooling, and a concrete agentic efficiency argument worth benchmarking against.
cards.summary: NVIDIA releases Nemotron 3 Embed: an 8B model scoring 78.5% on RTEB (ranked #1) and 75.5% on MMTEB Retrieval, plus 1B variants. The 1B BF16 cuts error rate 27–28% over its predecessor. Features include 32k context, multilingual/code retrieval, NVFP4 Blackwell quantization, and NeMo fine-tuning recipes. Stronger retrieval shown to reduce downstream agentic token cost.

cards.title: Google DeepMind & Isomorphic Labs Publish Joint Bioresilience AI Strategy
cards.why_it_matters: This outlines how frontier lab tooling — AlphaFold, AlphaGenome, AlphaEvolve, IsoDDE — is being operationalized for biosecurity, signaling a major applied direction for DeepMind's model stack.
cards.summary: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs detail a bioresilience program spanning prevention, detection, and response. Efforts include adapting SynthID watermarking to screen AI-generated biological sequences, using AlphaEvolve to optimize metagenomic sequencing pipelines, and granting trusted researchers access to AI systems for vaccine/countermeasure design. Over 15 government and biosecurity partnerships established in the past 12 months.

cards.title: DharmaOCR Beats Mistral OCR4 on Brazilian Portuguese via SFT + DPO Specialization
cards.why_it_matters: A clean case study of domain-specialized training outperforming larger general models — the SFT+DPO recipe and the neuron superposition / parameter allocation argument are applicable to any targeted fine-tuning decision.
cards.summary: DharmaOCR, fine-tuned specifically for Brazilian Portuguese OCR, outperforms Mistral OCR4 and Unlimited-OCR on a Portuguese-focused benchmark. Training used two stages: supervised fine-tuning on Portuguese documents for domain alignment, then Direct Preference Optimization to suppress degeneration and improve inference stability. The authors argue full parameter concentration on a restricted domain structurally advantages specialization over multilingual generalist models.

cards.title: Thinking Machines Lab Releases Inkling: 975B MoE Open-Weights Multimodal Model with 1M Context
cards.why_it_matters: This is a notable open-weights release from a newer lab — a 975B total / 41B active MoE pretrained on 45T tokens of text, image, audio, and video, positioned as a fine-tunable base, which the field will be evaluating.
cards.summary: Thinking Machines Lab releases Inkling, a Mixture-of-Experts model with 975B total and 41B active parameters, 1M-token context, pretrained on 45T multimodal tokens. A smaller Inkling-Small (12B active) is previewed. The model is designed for customization via their Tinker platform and ranks among strong open-weights models on agentic benchmarks including Design Arena. Not claimed as the strongest overall model.

cards.title: RoboTTT Scales Robot Policy Context to 8K Timesteps via Test-Time Training, +87% Over Single-Step Baseline
cards.why_it_matters: Test-Time Training as a mechanism for compressing long visuomotor histories into fast weights is a technically novel scaling axis for robot foundation models — directly relevant to researchers working on long-horizon agents or VLA policies.
cards.summary: RoboTTT integrates Test-Time Training into Vision-Language-Action robot policies, scaling visuomotor context to 8K timesteps without increasing inference latency. The recurrent state consists of fast weights updated by gradient descent during training and inference. On real-robot manipulation, it achieves 87% improvement over single-step context baselines and completes a ten-stage, five-minute assembly task that no baseline completes. Models pretrained at 8K context outperform 1K-context pretraining by 62%.

cards.title: LLMs Systematically Violate the Law of Total Probability: The 'Macro Fallacy'
cards.why_it_matters: This paper introduces a reference-free consistency criterion that exposes a fundamental probabilistic failure mode in frontier models — directly relevant to anyone building systems that rely on LLM probability estimates or population-level inference.
cards.summary: Researchers test whether LLM conditional estimates satisfy the law of total probability by recursively partitioning populations and aggregating subpopulation estimates. Across frontier models and problem domains, they find widespread violations. A key finding — the 'macro fallacy' — shows fine-grained subpopulation aggregates are often better-calibrated than direct population-level estimates, suggesting models hold relevant subpopulation knowledge but fail to propagate it upward.

cards.title: Decoupled Periodic TTT Updates Enable Real-Time Dynamic Novel View Synthesis with Long-Horizon Memory
cards.why_it_matters: The decoupled update-vs-apply frequency idea for TTT is a practically important efficiency insight applicable beyond NVS to any streaming scenario where gradient-based memory updates are too costly per frame.
cards.summary: The paper addresses the compute cost of per-frame Test-Time Training updates for online novel view synthesis from multi-view video. The proposed method decouples update frequency from application frequency: memory is updated periodically but applied per-frame via cross-view attention. An auxiliary Memory Loss prevents forgetting and a Memory Caching strategy regularizes weights against drift. The approach achieves real-time performance with state-of-the-art quality on dynamic human motion scenes.

cards.title: Deep Thermalisation Locality Bounded by Entanglement Teleportation, Timescale Scales Logarithmically
cards.why_it_matters: For researchers at the intersection of quantum computing and quantum information, this establishes a rigorous geometric bound connecting two quantum phenomena with implications for quantum circuit design and measurement-induced phase transitions.
cards.summary: The paper studies deep thermalisation — emergence of universal quantum state ensembles from projective measurements — in a bipartite geometry with causally disconnected subregions. It proves the onset is fundamentally bounded by measurement-induced entanglement teleportation. For generic locally interacting systems, both timescales scale logarithmically with subregion separation. Special circuits where measurement randomness is perfectly transmitted exhibit finite-time deep thermalisation and genuine non-locality.

cards.title: MeanFlowNFT: Forward-Process RL Fine-Tuning for Few-Step Flow Generators with Policy-Improvement Guarantee
cards.why_it_matters: This extends RL alignment (via DiffusionNFT's forward-process objective) to MeanFlow generators, with a formal guarantee and strong empirical results — relevant to researchers working on efficient generative model post-training.
cards.summary: MeanFlowNFT adapts the DiffusionNFT forward-process RL framework to MeanFlow models by constructing an instantaneous-velocity predictor from the MeanFlow identity, enabling reward optimization while preserving few-step sampling. It inherits DiffusionNFT's strict policy-improvement guarantee. On SD3.5-M, MeanFlowNFT outperforms prior SOTA RL-tuned few-step generators on 6 of 8 metrics. On Wan 2.1, 4-step MeanFlowNFT reaches VBench score 84.33, surpassing 50-step LongCat-Video RL (82.57).

cards.title: SciDiagramEdit: Benchmark for Instruction-Driven Figure Editing Mined from arXiv Revision Histories
cards.why_it_matters: Mining real author revision pairs from arXiv version histories is a clever self-supervised signal for scientific figure editing — relevant to researchers building multimodal agents or document AI tools.
cards.summary: SciDiagramEdit presents a benchmark and agentic learning framework for editing scientific figures under natural-language instructions. Training data is mined from before/after figure pairs in arXiv version histories. The agent operates on editable vector sources and uses a skill-evolution loop where an agentic proposer iteratively refines skill specifications from execution traces. Progressive skill refinement improves edit accuracy on a held-out validation set.
